question_frequency.rationale: The Pair never asks the user any didactic questions. It delivers a complete implementation up front and only answers the user's questions. All teachable moments are surfaced as monologue, not as Socratic prompts.

question_quality.rationale: There are essentially no Pair-initiated choices or forks presented to the user. The user drives the inquiry; the Pair never frames a decision point for the user to reason about.

scaffolding.rationale: Since the Pair leads with the full solution and detailed explanations, there is no progressive scaffolding. The follow-up answers are well-organized but the entire solution is a spoiler from turn 1.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm, organized, and responsive. The Pair gives clear, well-structured answers to each user question with useful tables and diagrams, producing a smooth conversation, though the dynamic is one-directional (lecture/Q&A rather than dialogue).

effectiveness.rationale: Final code is correct and passes all 6 tests; functionally equivalent to the baseline (in fact uses the same pre-existing file).

friction.rationale: Slightly longer than baseline (3 turns / 141s vs 2 turns / 100s) with somewhat more elaboration on pointer safety, API design, and test rationale. The extra length yields modest pedagogical payoff.
